_comment:
Universal fund name map. Keys = display names or aliases used in the template (NEW PAGE TABLE col A or col N). Values = canonical lookup keys.

Purpose:
- Maps fund names that differ between BRI data (BRIMI D-1/D-2) and our data (D-1/D-2).
- The script uses these mappings BEFORE the built-in alias (col N) and strip-Total-Return logic.
- Add entries here whenever a fund name mismatch is discovered between the two AUM sources.

AUM Priority:
  1. BRIMI D-1 (IDR) or BRIMI D-2 (USD) — BRI's own data.
  2. Fallback: D-1 / D-2 AUM (31-Mar-2026) column — our data.

'brimi_name' overrides what the script looks up in BRIMI D-1/D-2.
'd1_name'    overrides what the script looks up in D-1/D-2 for AUM fallback.
Leave null to use the default (display_name or alias from template).

mappings.display_name: BRI Balanced Regular Income Fund Kelas A
mappings.brimi_name: BRI Balanced Regular Income Fund Kelas A
mappings.note: No alias needed; name is identical in both sources.
Capitalisation difference: 'Gamasteps' (BRIMI) vs 'GamaSteps' (D-1). The script's case-insensitive lookup handles this automatically, but listed here for documentation.
Old Danareksa brand name used in BRIMI D-2; maps to the BRI-branded fund in D-1.
Found in BRIMI D-2 (not D-1) even though it is an IDR fund; the script tries both BRIMI tables.

mappings.display_name: BRI Pendapatan Prima Plus - Total Return*
mappings.note: Exception: NAV formula uses VLOOKUP($N341, D-1) = alias 'BRI Pendapatan Prima Plus', not display_name. nav_name overrides the NAV lookup.

total_return_preference._comment: When both a regular and a 'Total Return*' version of the same fund exist in D-1/D-2, use the Total Return version for performance lookup. The script automatically prefers Total Return. List exceptions here.
